segments.text: PART 1
WOMAN: Hi Coleman, how are you?
segments.translation: 女士：嗨，科尔曼，最近好吗？

segments.text: COLEMAN: Good, thanks.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：很好，谢谢。

segments.text: WOMAN: I wanted to have a chat with you because our friend Josh told me that you've joined a guitar group and it sounds interesting.
segments.translation: 女士：我想和你聊聊，因为我们的朋友乔希告诉我，你加入了一个吉他小组，听起来很有趣。

segments.text: I'd really like to learn myself.
segments.translation: 我真的很想自学一下。

segments.text: COLEMAN: Why don't you come along?
segments.translation: 科尔曼：你为什么不一起来学呢？

segments.text: I'm sure there's room for another person.
segments.translation: 我相信还能再加一个人。

segments.text: WOMAN: Really? So - who runs the classes?
segments.translation: 女士：真的吗？谁负责这些课程？

segments.text: COLEMAN: He's called a 'coordinator' - his name's Gary Mathieson.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：他被称为“协调员”，他的名字是加里·马蒂森。

segments.text: WOMAN: Let me note that down. Gary. How do you spell his surname?
segments.translation: 女士：让我记下来。加里，他的姓怎么拼？

segments.text: COLEMAN: It's M-A-T-H-I-E-S-O-N.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：是 M-A-T-H-I-E-S-O-N。

segments.text: WOMAN: Right, thanks.
segments.translation: 女士：好的，谢谢。

segments.text: COLEMAN: He's retired, actually, but he's a really nice guy and he used to play in a lot of bands.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：他其实已经退休了，但人很好，以前参加过很多乐队。

segments.text: WOMAN: Thanks. So how long have you been going?
segments.translation: 女士：谢谢，你去多久了？

segments.text: COLEMAN: About a month now.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：大概一个月了

segments.text: WOMAN: And could you play anything before you started?
segments.translation: 女士：开始学之前你会弹些什么呢？

segments.text: COLEMAN: I knew a few chords, but that's all.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：我会几个和弦，仅此而已。

segments.text: WOMAN: I'm sure everyone will be better than me.
segments.translation: 女士：我相信每个人都比我弹得好。

segments.text: COLEMAN: That's what I thought, too.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：我一开始也是这样想的。

segments.text: When I first spoke to Gary on the phone, he said it was a class for beginners, but I was still worried that everyone would be better than me, but we were all equally hopeless!
segments.translation: 我第一次和加里通电话时，他说这是为初学者开设的课程，但我还是担心每个人都会比我强，但我们都一样一窍不通！

segments.text: WOMAN: Oh, that's reassuring. So where do you meet?
segments.translation: 女士：那我就放心了，你们会在哪里见？

segments.text: COLEMAN: Well, when I joined the group, they were meeting in Gary's home, but as the group got bigger, he decided to book a room at the college in town. I prefer going there.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：嗯，当我加入这个小组时，他们在加里的家里集合，但是随着小组越来越大，他决定在城里的大学预定一个房间。我更喜欢去那里。

segments.text: WOMAN: I know that place. I used to go to tap dancing classes there when I was at secondary school.
segments.translation: 女士：我知道那个地方。我上中学的时候常去那里上踢踏舞课。

segments.text: I haven't been since, though and I can't remember what road it's in . . . is it Lock Street?
segments.translation: 从那以后我就没去过了，我不记得是在哪条路上了，是洛克街吗？

segments.text: COLEMAN: It's just beyond there at the bottom of New Street near the city roundabout.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：就在那边的新街尽头，在城市环岛附近。

segments.text: WOMAN: Yes, of course.
segments.translation: 女士：对，没错。

segments.text: COLEMAN: The guitar club is on the first floor in Room T347.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：吉他俱乐部在一楼的T347室。

segments.text: WOMAN: Right. And when do you meet? Is it at the weekend?
segments.translation: 女士：好的，你们什么时候见面，周末吗？

segments.text: COLEMAN: We meet on Thursdays. It used to be 10.30 and that suited me well, but now we meet at 11.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：我们每周四见。以前是十点半，我觉得很合适，但现在是十一点。

segments.text: The class that's in there before us asked if they could have the room for another 30 minutes.
segments.translation: 在我们之前的同学问他们能不能再占用这个房间30分钟。

segments.text: WOMAN: Oh, I see. Well, I'd love to come, but I don't have a guitar.
segments.translation: 女士：哦，我知道了。我很想来，但我没有吉他。

segments.text: COLEMAN: Well, you can always buy a second-hand one.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：你可以买一把二手吉他。

segments.text: There's a website called 'The perfect instrument' that sells all kinds of guitars, violins and so on. I'm sure you'll find something there.
segments.translation: 有个网站叫 "完美乐器"，出售各种吉他、小提琴等。我相信你会在那里找到的。

segments.text: WOMAN: So what's a typical lesson like with Gary?
segments.translation: 女士：那么，加里平常的课程是怎么样呢？

segments.text: COLEMAN: Well, he always starts by getting us to tune our guitars. That takes about five minutes.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：嗯，他总是先让我们给吉他调音。大约五分钟时间。

segments.text: WOMAN: Uhuh.
segments.translation: 女士：嗯嗯。

segments.text: COLEMAN: Some people have an app they use, but others do it by ear.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：有些人会用自己的应用程序，有些人用自己的耳朵听。

segments.text: Gary goes round and helps them. And while he's doing that, he tells us what he's going to do during the lesson.
segments.translation: 加里会过去帮助他们。他一边调一边告诉我们上课要做什么。

segments.text: WOMAN: Right.
segments.translation: 女士：好的。

segments.text: COLEMAN: First, we usually spend about ten minutes doing some strumming.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：首先，我们会花十分钟左右的时间来弹奏。

segments.text: WOMAN: So is that using . . . what are they called . . . plectrums?
segments.translation: 女士：那是不是用什么，叫拨子？

segments.text: COLEMAN: No - we just use our thumbs.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：不，我们就用手指。

segments.text: WOMAN: Much easier.
segments.translation: 女士：那简单多了。

segments.text: COLEMAN: Gary reminds us where to put our fingers for each chord and then we play them together.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：加里提醒我们每个和弦的手指位置，然后我们一起弹奏。

segments.text: Sometimes we all just start laughing because we're so bad at keeping time, so Gary starts clapping to help us.
segments.translation: 有时我们都会笑起来，因为我们太不会掌控节奏，加里就开始拍手帮助我们。

segments.text: WOMAN: Do you learn to play any songs?
segments.translation: 女士：你们会学弹奏任何歌曲吗？

segments.text: COLEMAN: Yes - we do at least one song with words and chords. I mean that's harder than you think.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：是的，我们至少会弹一首有歌词与和弦的歌。这比你想象的要难一些。

segments.text: WOMAN: Oh, I'm sure it is!
segments.translation: 女士：哦，我想是的。

segments.text: COLEMAN: That part of the lesson takes about 15 minutes.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：这部分大约需要十五分钟。

segments.text: He often brings a recording of the song and plays it to us first.
segments.translation: 他通常会把这首歌的录音带过来，先给我们播放一遍。

segments.text: Then he hands out the song and if there's a new chord in it, we practise that before we play it together - but really slowly.
segments.translation: 然后，他把歌曲发给我们，如果其中有新的和弦，我们就先练习一下，然后再一起弹奏——但弹得很慢。

segments.text: WOMAN: Do you do any finger picking?
segments.translation: 女士：你们会进行指弹练习吗？

segments.text: COLEMAN:  That's the last ten minutes of the lesson, when we pick out the individual notes from a tune he's made up. It's always quite simple.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：那是课程的最后十分钟，我们从他编的曲子中挑出单个的音符。总是很简单的。

segments.text: WOMAN:   That must be hard, though.
segments.translation: 女士：那一定挺累人的。

segments.text: COLEMAN:  It is, but people like it because they can really concentrate and if we're all playing well, it sounds quite impressive.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：是的，但人们喜欢这样，因为他们可以真正集中精力，而且如果我们都弹得很好，听起来就会很印象深刻。

segments.text: The only trouble is that he sometimes gets us to play one at a time - you know, alone.
segments.translation: 唯一的问题是，他有时会让我们自己弹--你知道，单独演奏。

segments.text: WOMAN:   That's scary.
segments.translation: 女人：那有些可怕。

segments.text: COLEMAN:  It is, but I've got used to it now.
segments.translation: 科尔曼：是的，但我现在已经习惯了。

segments.text: At the end he spends about five minutes telling us what to practise for the following week.
segments.translation: 最后他还会花五分钟告诉我们下周要练什么。

segments.text: WOMAN:   Well, thanks Coleman. I'll go and have a look at that website, I think.
segments.translation: 女士：谢谢你，科尔曼。我想我会去看看那个网站的。
